quality_highlights: engines.md: Exemplary reference doc. Feature comparison table, timeout knob table, per-engine guidance with copy-paste YAML, and 'Which engine should I choose?' decision prose. A model for reference documentation quality.

permissions_validation.go: formatMissingPermissionsMessage provides clear two-option fix path with inline YAML snippet and docs URL. ValidateIncludedPermissions error message names the exact fix location (frontmatter) and shows ready-to-paste YAML. Strong error-message craftsmanship.
ci-doctor.md: Medical persona is applied consistently across all messages (🩺🏥, 'reporting for duty', 'examining the patient', 'diagnosis', 'Prescription issued 💊'). Persona coherence is excellent.

single_file_improvements.issue: Guide covers only Tavily MCP configuration and omits Codex's native opt-in web-search tool. engines.md feature table notes 'tools.web-search: ✅ (opt-in)' for Codex but the web-search guide has no mention of this. Enterprise users picking Codex follow the cross-reference to this guide and find no guidance for their engine. The intro phrase 'While alternatives exist (Exa, SerpAPI, Brave Search), this guide focuses on Tavily' dismisses provider alternatives without covering the fundamentally different native-vs-MCP distinction.

run-failure message ends with 'Structure status unknown...' - vague trailing ellipsis creates an incomplete feeling compared to ci-doctor's 'Doctor needs assistance' which at least implies escalation. More importantly, run-success duplicates meaning: 'Architecture scan complete' and 'has reviewed code structure' convey the same thing in sequence, and the final '📋' after 'Report delivered' is decorative rather than informative.
